compositional_truth_check.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: Pot is present but contains no visible foliage.
compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No evidence of mint foliage; pot appears empty or dormant.
compositional_truth_check.P3_Pothos: Present: Single leaf specimen remains with white rabbit anchor.
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No distinct specimen identified.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is experiencing severe attrition. Only one Pothos leaf remains viable. The yellow pot (P1) and black pot (P2/P4) show no active growth.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: One Pothos leaf, showing signs of chlorosis/thinning.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2, no distinct plant detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, appears to have organic debris (eggshells) added as a soil amendment.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a small electronic component (IC chip/header) introduced as an anomaly.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_Current: The Pothos leaf in P3 has maintained its position but shows a slight decline in turgor. The surrounding pots have remained static and devoid of visible plant life throughout the observation window.

visual_health_inference.P3_Pothos: Stressed. The leaf is pale and lacks the structural integrity of a healthy specimen.

anomalies: Introduction of electronic component (IC header) on the desk surface.
Use of crushed eggshells as a soil additive in P3.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced decline. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it appears fragile. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively barren, suggesting either total plant loss or extreme dormancy. The introduction of non-botanical debris (eggshells and electronics) suggests an experimental or neglected environment.
